que: In State Hoisting, what is the primary goal?
options: replace testing entirely
skip state/data modeling
move state to caller for reuse/testability
avoid understanding requirements

que: Which API/concept is most central to State Hoisting?
options: manual polling loop for all tasks
random utility class name
legacy XML-only patterns unrelated to this topic
value + onValueChange

que: What is a recommended best practice for State Hoisting?
options: optimize before verifying correctness
mix unrelated responsibilities in one function
ignore edge/error states
separate stateful and stateless composables

que: Which is a common mistake when implementing State Hoisting?
options: burying all state deep in leaf UI
keep boundaries clear
write focused tests for behavior
follow official docs for API usage

que: For production code, which approach fits State Hoisting best?
options: Use State Hoisting with clear state flow and testable logic
Use State Hoisting while bypassing architecture boundaries
Use State Hoisting only as theory with no implementation
Use State Hoisting and suppress all warnings

que: A teammate asks where to start with State Hoisting. What is best advice?
options: copy an unknown snippet without context
avoid feedback from tests/review
skip running the app after changes
Read official docs (https://developer.android.com/develop/ui/compose/state-hoisting) and implement a small sample

que: Which statement about State Hoisting is most accurate?
options: State Hoisting is mainly about adding boilerplate
State Hoisting removes the need for any testing
State Hoisting is most effective when paired with clear ownership of state/data
State Hoisting should be applied identically in every layer

que: In a code review for State Hoisting, what would you flag?
options: traceable data flow between layers
explicit modeling of UI/data states
small pure functions with clear names
Code that hides failures instead of handling them explicitly

que: Which scenario is the strongest example of using State Hoisting correctly?
options: a large refactor without tests
a slide deck with no runnable code
an implementation with no error states
A small, working feature that demonstrates value + onValueChange in context

que: To learn State Hoisting deeply, what should you do next?
options: avoid official references
Build, test, profile (if needed), and iterate using State Hoisting patterns
memorize definitions only
skip verification to save time
